intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Rubellia aurea. However, I can suggest that you might be referring to Rubia tinctorum, commonly known as madder or Rubia plant, or possibly Rubia cordifolia, a different species of the Rubiaceae family.

If you are referring to Rubia tinctorum, here is a paragraph about the plant:

 Rubia tinctorum, also known as madder, is a perennial plant species native to the Old World, valued for its vibrant red, pink, and purple dyes. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help in wound healing, reducing fever, and treating various gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Rubia tinctorum include anthraquinones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Rubia tinctorum is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as to produce the madder lake, a natural dye used in traditional medicine and as a coloring agent in food and cosmetics.
